title: Pre-Call Briefing
description: Generate a pre-call briefing with company research, value hypotheses, and discovery questions, then review your briefing history.

steps.narration: The pre-call briefing page helps you prepare for upcoming meetings. Right now there's no briefing generated yet, so you see the empty state with a prompt to get started.
The Generate Briefing area lets you specify the buyer's role and meeting context. This helps the AI tailor its research and recommendations to your specific situation.

callout.text: Provide context for a tailored briefing

steps.narration: Click Generate to start the briefing process. The AI will research the company, analyze the deal context, and produce actionable preparation materials.
The briefing is being generated. This typically takes thirty to sixty seconds as the AI pulls company data, analyzes your deal history, and drafts personalized recommendations.
The briefing is complete. Let's walk through each section. At the top is the company research summary with industry position, recent news, and competitive context.
The company research section covers Meridian Dynamics' position as a mid-market technology consulting firm, their recent expansion of cloud practice, and competitive landscape against larger firms.
Scroll down to the value hypotheses section. These are data-backed talking points tailored to what matters most to Meridian Dynamics based on the deal context.
Each value hypothesis is specific and actionable. Legacy ERP modernization savings, unified data platform benefits, and a cloud migration path aligned with their growth plans.
The discovery questions section gives you conversation starters designed to uncover the buyer's priorities and decision criteria.
These questions are tailored to Meridian's situation. What's driving the transformation timeline, how are teams sharing customer data, and what does success look like for Q3.
Navigate to your briefing history to see past preparations. Each entry shows the date, buyer role, and meeting context so you can track your preparation across multiple meetings.
The history list shows three past briefings with different buyer roles and dates. Click any entry to review the full briefing content from that preparation session.
